campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell city council met to discuss several community improvements, including accepting a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. The council also worked on plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue and voted on extending a temporary local ordinance.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Plan to build a bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bicycle and pedestrian infrastructure study
Extension of interim ordinance 2326

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met to decide which community groups and events should receive grant money from the city. They approved the final list of organizations getting funding, decided how much money each group would get, and made some clarifications to the rules for how groups can apply for event grants.
keyTopics: Approved which community organizations will receive city grant funding
Set specific dollar amounts for individual grant recipients
Approved funding for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated rules and clarifications for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information on how residents can watch the proceedings through multiple platforms including cable TV, online streaming, and phone dial-in options. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages to help residents follow along, though this particular agenda focused mainly on viewing and participation logistics rather than specific policy items.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, website, and Zoom
Live translations available in 50+ languages for accessibility
Phone dial-in option for residents without internet access
No public comment period listed for this agenda

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. Residents who were planning to attend or submit comments about zoning issues will need to wait for the next hearing, which is rescheduled for April 8, 2026.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Next hearing moved to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and discuss applications for federal Community Development Block Grant funding, which helps pay for housing and community programs in Sunnyvale. The group also approved meeting minutes from their previous session in February.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding for housing and community development projects
Review of CDBG funding applications from local organizations
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching a license amnesty program to get unregistered businesses on the books, promoting a 'Shop Local' campaign for summer, and offering financial incentives to encourage retail stores and startups to set up shop in Cupertino. The city is also partnering with private companies to sponsor community events.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - getting unregistered businesses to formally register
Shop Local summer campaign to encourage residents to support Cupertino businesses
Tax incentives and financial help for retail stores, small businesses, and startups
Outreach to shopping centers to increase participation in city programs
Public-private partnerships to fund and sponsor local events

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to handle some rescheduled items and address two main issues: a weed abatement program to clean up overgrown weeds around the city, and a plan for how to spend federal housing grants in 2026-27. Residents had the chance to speak up about the housing funding plan.
keyTopics: 2026 County Weed Abatement Program - removing weeds on residential and commercial properties
Federal HUD housing grant spending plan for 2026-27 - public comment period
Multiple items postponed or reconsidered from previous meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine items, including honoring Women's History Month with a proclamation. The council approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software services costing about $93,000 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency disaster relief agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
City facilities designated for disaster response use
